===Pokémon===
[[File:Roy party.png|thumb|250px|Roy's current [[party]]]]
Roy's team consists mainly of gluttons, which is a trait their Trainer's also has, who had problems in the past, usually involving some form of insecurity: {{TP|Roy|Skeledirge}} was initially embarrassed of singing in front of others, {{TP|Roy|Kilowattrel}} was scared of heights, and {{TP|Roy|Lucario}} would easily get frustrated. However, through his unorthodox methods, he was able to help his Pokémon overcome them and, by doing so, allowed them to truly grow. During Skeledirge and Kilowattrel's first stages, they used to look up to [[Friede's Charizard]] and [[Captain Pikachu]], respectively, due to their shared types. His Pokémon all share Roy's eagerness for battling, as they don't give up until the end.

Furthermore, his Pokémon seemed to take on a particular role on the team as they developed: Skeledirge and {{p|Lucario}} are Roy's main {{pkmn|battle}}rs, with Lucario's [[Mega Evolution]] in particular acting as the team's power equalizer against tougher opponents, while Kilowattrel is the secondary battler, although it mainly acts as aerial support. When Captain Pikachu was traveling with Roy, he often contributed in tough battles with his greater experience and power. Roy's Pokémon seem to have a tight knit bond with each other and especially work well together as a unit due to their extensive training in [[Kalos]].

In battle, Roy makes good use of his Pokémon's moves and unique abilities, such as Kilowattrel being able to confound its opponents as it flies through the air and Lucario's Mega-Evolved forms, as seen in ''[[HZ134|One Step Closer]]'' when he had it use its head appendages as weapons to fight [[Chalce]]'s Mega {{p|Medicham}}. He also makes great use of Skeledirge's singing voice through {{m|Disarming Voice}} and {{m|Torch Song}}.
